worldview: Keep the published pr1394 SP8192/GPTQ-embeddings/depth-recurrence/MuonEq-R/SDClip stack frozen and treat this cycle as a harness-validity search over bounded comparison, selector, and transition programs. Favor families that change the unit of comparison or artifact/transition policy on the real base, and mark stateful controller or branching lines as blocked when the current target script and harness do not yet expose the needed primitives.

hypotheses.name: Control-Paired Delta Admission On Downstream Sliding Score
hypotheses.mechanism: Replace raw ranking by control-paired delta ranking: each candidate is admitted and promoted only by its improvement over matched controls on downstream quantized sliding-window `score_bpb`, with pre-quant `val_bpb` used only as a guardrail rather than as a co-equal ranking metric.
hypotheses.broken_invariant: Absolute downstream means from a fresh pr1394 pack are stable enough to rank families without explicitly estimating pack-specific control spread.
hypotheses.not_local_tuning_reason: This changes the unit of comparison for the whole campaign and therefore which families survive at all; it does not retune any model scalar inside the pr1394 stack.
hypotheses.measurement_plan: Replay-equivalent controls establish a tight delta band, and real winners exceed that band on downstream `score_bpb` even when pre-quant improvements are smaller.
hypotheses.smallest_decisive_probe: Run one 8-slot admission pack with 2 exact controls, 2 replay-equivalent control clones, and 4 novel candidates. Promote only if a novel candidate clears the control-clone noise floor on downstream `score_bpb`.
hypotheses.expected_observable: Replay-equivalent controls establish a tight delta band, and real winners exceed that band on downstream `score_bpb` even when pre-quant improvements are smaller.
hypotheses.falsifier: If control clones rank as winners or their spread is comparable to the claimed gains from novel candidates, the pack is not learning enough to justify family promotion.
hypotheses.novelty_vs_history: This is distinct from prior patch-pack search because it changes campaign comparison semantics instead of introducing another model edit. It directly addresses the recorded failure mode of single-metric ranking under unstable pack conditions.
hypotheses.self_check_summary: Consequential because it changes campaign admission, not a local threshold. The probe is cheap and decisive, the lane is explicitly downstream, and the executability claim stays within control/candidate packing the harness already appears to support.

hypotheses.name: Artifact Selector Family Over The Same Training Trace
hypotheses.mechanism: Search over selector programs that choose which already-trained checkpoint/export state becomes the artifact: last-step export, best pre-quant checkpoint, post-warmdown checkpoint, or stability-window checkpoint, all under the fixed pr1394 GPTQ+SDClip deployment path.
hypotheses.broken_invariant: The final training state is automatically the best downstream sliding-window artifact on the pr1394 base.
hypotheses.not_local_tuning_reason: The family changes which trained state is exported and compared; that is an artifact-selection hypothesis, not a scalar retune within one export recipe.
hypotheses.measurement_plan: At least one non-final selector beats the published last-step-style control on downstream `score_bpb`, possibly with similar or slightly worse pre-quant `val_bpb`.
hypotheses.smallest_decisive_probe: From one shared training trace, export 3 selector-defined checkpoints plus a matched final-step control and compare downstream quantized sliding-window `score_bpb` with pre-quant `val_bpb` as secondary evidence.
hypotheses.expected_observable: At least one non-final selector beats the published last-step-style control on downstream `score_bpb`, possibly with similar or slightly worse pre-quant `val_bpb`.
hypotheses.falsifier: If all selector-defined exports collapse within control spread or the best pre-quant checkpoint consistently underperforms on downstream score, the selector family is false for this base.
hypotheses.novelty_vs_history: Distinct from optimizer or architecture mutation because it attacks the artifact-selection contract directly. It is also aligned with the repo finding that the searchable object should include selector programs, not just training patches.
hypotheses.self_check_summary: This is a first-order artifact-selection family with a same-trace probe and a crisp falsifier. It stays honest by keeping the published training base fixed and only varying which state becomes the candidate artifact.

hypotheses.name: State-Triggered Recurrence Handoff Program
hypotheses.mechanism: Replace the single fixed recurrence schedule with a bounded handoff program: keep the non-looped trunk until cheap training signals indicate stabilization, then enable depth recurrence together with its finisher regime so recurrent compute is allocated only when the base representation is ready.
hypotheses.broken_invariant: The best way to use recurrence on pr1394 is a fixed wall-clock fraction such as `ENABLE_LOOPING_AT`, rather than a state-triggered transition program.
hypotheses.not_local_tuning_reason: This family changes when recurrent compute exists and why the optimizer crosses regimes; it rewrites the training program around a handoff, rather than nudging one schedule scalar.
hypotheses.measurement_plan: A real handoff program should show a cleaner post-transition pre-quant slope and then a better downstream sliding-window score at similar wall-clock budget.
hypotheses.smallest_decisive_probe: Compare three programs: fixed published recurrence control, late handoff by coarse phase rule, and signal-triggered handoff using cheap observables such as loss slope or update norm. Judge first by post-handoff pre-quant trajectory and finally by downstream `score_bpb`.
hypotheses.expected_observable: A real handoff program should show a cleaner post-transition pre-quant slope and then a better downstream sliding-window score at similar wall-clock budget.
hypotheses.falsifier: If signal-triggered handoff is indistinguishable from the fixed control within control spread, or it destabilizes immediately after transition, the mechanism is false.
hypotheses.novelty_vs_history: This line is different from a bare `ENABLE_LOOPING_AT` sweep because the object of search is the handoff policy itself, matching the repo's recurring controller/handoff abstraction rather than another static threshold pack.
hypotheses.self_check_summary: Strong consequence and novelty because it turns static recurrence timing into a transition program. It remains honest by marking the idea blocked until the target script or harness exposes runtime state-conditioned control hooks.

hypotheses.name: Shared-Trunk Late-Branch Portfolio
hypotheses.mechanism: Train a shared pr1394 trunk once, then fork a small portfolio of late finishers or export selectors near warmdown and pick the best downstream artifact from the branch set instead of committing to one endgame path for the whole run.
hypotheses.broken_invariant: One committed finisher is the right compute topology; late branching cannot produce better downstream artifacts per unit compute than independent single-path runs.
hypotheses.not_local_tuning_reason: This changes the topology of the run by reallocating compute from full independent candidates to a shared-trunk plus portfolio program, which is a composition-level search move rather than a parameter tweak.
hypotheses.measurement_plan: At least one late branch beats the single-path control on downstream `score_bpb` by more than control spread while consuming less total compute than training the same number of full independent candidates.
hypotheses.smallest_decisive_probe: Run 2 controls and up to 4 branches off the same trunk checkpoint, where each branch differs only in finisher/export policy. Compare marginal downstream gain per additional finisher budget against a single-path control.
hypotheses.expected_observable: At least one late branch beats the single-path control on downstream `score_bpb` by more than control spread while consuming less total compute than training the same number of full independent candidates.
hypotheses.falsifier: If branch winners never clear control spread, or branch-specific variance dominates any apparent gain, the portfolio story is false on this base.
hypotheses.novelty_vs_history: Distinct from both selector-only and handoff-only families because the searchable object is a branch portfolio over a shared trunk. It maps directly to the repo's recurring late-branch abstraction instead of another static slate.
hypotheses.self_check_summary: This is consequential because it changes compute topology and artifact selection together. The probe is still compact, but the current harness likely lacks shared-trunk branch orchestration, so the blocked classification is required.

rejected_lines: Retune `ENABLE_LOOPING_AT`, `WARMDOWN_FRAC`, or `WARMUP_STEPS` by themselves; that is within-family scalar exploitation, not a consequential hypothesis family.
Sweep `MUON_MOMENTUM`, `MUON_BETA2`, or `GRAD_CLIP_NORM` around the published base; there is no first-order mechanism shift here.
Change only the SDClip multiplier or GPTQ micro-settings on the fixed pr1394 stack; that is deployment micro-tuning unless promoted into a broader artifact-selection or deployment-family story.
Vary tokenizer or vocab size inside this cycle; that is a base-contract move and violates the requested fixed-pr1394 comparison base.
